Dry-style Thai noodle with grilled chicken

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Thai dry grilled chicken noodle. FOODON_00002473 (noodle dish) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Noodles, cooked) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for Thai dry grilled chicken noodle.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Thai noodle dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of dry grilled chicken noodle, approximately 350 g, comprising a portion of springy egg noodles tossed in savory sauce and topped with sliced grilled chicken.

nutrient_source.title: Noodles with meat, mixed dish — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data and FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition tables for Thai noodle dishes
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FAO INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Thai dry grilled chicken noodle exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked egg noodles (FDC ID 168936), grilled chicken breast (FDC ID 171477), and typical Thai sauce components (soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil) weighted to a representative 100 g portion of the assembled dish. FAO/INFOODS SEA tables used to cross-check plausibility for Southeast Asian noodle dishes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of egg noodles and mixed noodle dishes — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Thai dry grilled chicken noodle. GI of 47 estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for egg noodles (GI ~47, low category) as the primary carbohydrate vehicle. The addition of grilled chicken protein and sauce components is expected to maintain or slightly lower the effective GI. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (47 × 64.75) / 100 ≈ 30.4, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 18.5 × 3.5 = 64.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce and oyster sauce
hypertension.guidance: Each 350 g serving provides approximately 1470 mg sodium, representing about 64% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2300 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sauce preparation or limit portion size to manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from egg noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 47 (low) but a glycemic load of approximately 30 per standard serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and pair this dish with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from sauce components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content (~1470 mg per serving) and moderate saturated fat (~3.2 g per serving) are relevant for cardiovascular risk management; grilled rather than fried chicken preparation is a favorable attribute, but sauce reduction is advisable for individuals with established cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dry Grilled Chicken Noodle (Thai Style), known in Thai as บะหมี่ไก่ย่างแห้ง (Ba-mi kai yang haeng), is a Thai noodle dish consisting of springy egg noodles tossed in a savory-sweet sauce made from soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil, topped with slices of marinated grilled chicken breast. Unlike soup-based noodle dishes, the 'dry' preparation means the noodles are drained and coated in sauce rather than served in broth, resulting in a concentrated, umami-rich flavor profile with a slightly chewy texture from the egg noodles and a smoky, tender quality from the grilled chicken. The dish is commonly garnished with spring onions, bean sprouts, and sometimes a wedge of lime. In Singapore, this dish is served at Thai restaurants such as Saap Saap Thai and is listed on menus as 'N5 Dry Grilled Chicken Noodle'. Nutritionally, a standard 350 g serving provides approximately 518 kcal, 35.7 g protein, 13.3 g fat, and 64.8 g carbohydrate. The protein content from grilled chicken makes it a satisfying meal, while the egg noodle base contributes a low-to-moderate glycemic index of approximately 47. Sodium content is elevated due to soy and oyster sauces (~1470 mg per serving), which is a key consideration for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is dairy-free and contains common allergens including gluten, egg, soy, and sesame.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Thai dry grilled chicken noodle was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA tables. Nutrient values were estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked egg noodles and grilled chicken breast with typical Thai sauce component weights. GI was estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for egg noodles as the closest analogous carbohydrate source. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level data sources are high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy or FAO/INFOODS SEA source data for this dish type. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g; this is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for egg noodles and grilled chicken breast with no hydrogenated fat components. Serving size of 350 g is estimated based on typical Thai restaurant noodle dish portions; actual restaurant portion may vary. The food_group field from the input ('Meat and alternatives') has been preserved as it reflects the protein-dominant character of the dish, though 'Mixed' could also be argued given the noodle base. Halal status is marked false as it cannot be confirmed without restaurant certification; the dish may be halal-certifiable depending on the restaurant. The menu item code 'N5' is a restaurant-specific identifier for Saap Saap Thai - Funan and is included in aliases for retrieval purposes.
